commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the Control UI favicon, touch icon, ICO, and SVG logo assets with AI-generated 3D lobster artwork under the existing public filenames.

PR surface: Source -19. Total -19 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a user-visible visual asset replacement rather than a bug report. The relevant check is proof of the changed browser, login/logo, PWA, and ICO surfaces, which the updated screenshot now supplies.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is the right lane because the remaining blocker is maintainer design/product acceptance, not a narrow code defect automation can repair.

target.title: improve: ship 3D rendered lobster favicon for Control UI
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Control UI uses a flat, hand-drawn 2D lobster as its favicon and logo. This PR upgrades that mascot to a 3D-rendered version of the **same lobster**, giving the browser tab icon, the login screen logo, and the installed PWA icon a more polished, modern look without changing the brand identity.

## Why This Change Was Made

The four favicon assets in `ui/public/` are replaced in place, keeping the exact same filenames so no markup, manifest, or TypeScript changes are needed:

- `favicon.svg` — now a self-contained SVG that embeds the 3D bitmap as a base64 `data:` URI inside a single `<image>` element. Because the login page and agent logo already reference `favicon.svg`, they pick up the new art automatically.
- `favicon-32.png` — 32×32 raster of the 3D lobster.
- `apple-touch-icon.png` — 180×180 raster.
- `favicon.ico` — regenerated from the 3D art.

No external assets are introduced; `favicon.svg` remains a single, dependency-free file. `index.html`, `manifest.webmanifest`, and all referencing TS modules are untouched.

## User Impact

Users see the upgraded 3D lobster in their browser tab favicon, on the login screen, and as the installed PWA / Apple touch icon. No behavioral changes.

## Evidence

Before / after render of `favicon.svg` (2D vs 3D), both on a transparent background:

![favicon before and after](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/woohahahaaa/openclaw/pr-assets/pr-assets/favicon-before-after.png)

Local validation:

- `pnpm build` — passed (favicon assets correctly copied into `dist/control-ui/`).
- `pnpm check` — passed except for the pre-existing `npm-shrinkwrap.json is stale` guard on `main`, which is unrelated to this change (no dependency or `package.json` edits were made; `npm-shrinkwrap.json` is untouched).
- Targeted favicon tests passed: `src/ui/public-assets.test.ts`, `src/ui/views/agents-utils.test.ts`.

## AI assistance

This change is **AI-assisted**. The 3D lobster artwork was generated with an AI image tool, and the asset pipeline (resizing to favicon sizes, embedding the bitmap into a self-contained SVG, ico generation) was carried out by an AI agent. The contributor confirms understanding of the change: it only swaps four static favicon assets in `ui/public/` with no code-path changes.

---

## Update — addressing Codex review (commit `9ce2bae`)

**1. `favicon.ico` regression — fixed.** The previous PR head encoded `favicon.ico` with only a single `(32,32)` entry, while `main` ships `[(16,16),(32,32),(48,48),(64,64),(128,128)]`. The ICO has been regenerated from the 256×256 source art and now matches `main` exactly with all five sizes.

**2. Real running-UI evidence — added.** The fork branch was cloned into an isolated temp dir, frontend deps installed, and the Vite dev server started. Before/after screenshots were captured from the **actually running Control UI dev server**, covering the three surfaces Codex called out — browser tab favicon, login-page logo, and PWA / Apple touch icon — plus a full 5-size `favicon.ico` breakdown:

![real running UI before/after](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/woohahahaaa/openclaw/pr-assets/pr-assets/favicon-before-after-real.png)

The remaining open item is the **art-direction sign-off** on the 3D mascot, which requires maintainer design approval.
